publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, canonical fill-gap, and no code task for JShell experiments; every item remains blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: JShell experiments: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? If an expression succeeds once in a long-running JShell session, the same text is automatically a complete reproducible Java program.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use JShell for a bounded experiment, then transfer the finding into saved source with explicit imports, declarations, inputs, and a repeatable check.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Use JShell for a bounded experiment, then transfer the finding into saved source with explicit imports, declarations, inputs, and a repeatable check.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Use JShell for a bounded experiment, then transfer the finding into saved source with explicit imports, declarations, inputs, and a repeatable check.

items.title: JShell experiments: canonical checkpoint
items.prompt: Complete the source-aligned Java: Beginner to Intermediate correction with the exact canonical phrase used by this lesson.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint phrase from the operational correction, then read the complete sentence for semantic fit.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'bounded experiment'. Use JShell for a bounded experiment, then transfer the finding into saved source with explicit imports, declarations, inputs, and a repeatable check.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the exact language rule. Use JShell for a bounded experiment, then transfer the finding into saved source with explicit imports, declarations, inputs, and a repeatable check.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical checkpoint phrase that completes the source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and whether additional spelling variants are technically equivalent.
